name: User starts a voice memo, mid-sentence taps Cancel — nothing is sent
description: Tests the cancel path on the recording panel. Cancel must teardown MediaRecorder + the WS to AssemblyAI + the AudioContext, drop accumulated audio chunks before the onstop handler tries to upload them, and return the composer to idle with the previous textarea draft preserved.

persona.name: Diego
persona.role: Realised mid-sentence he was about to send the wrong quote
persona.context: Said 'wait, I'm thinking of a different job' and changed his mind.

preconditions: Conversation already has some history — user has a saved draft 'Reminder for the Hernandez deck' in the textarea

steps.expect: Recording panel mounts; LIVE pill pulsing; timer at 0:00

steps.action: Speaks for ~3s ('quote a fence for the Pat— wait')
steps.expect: Live transcript shows 'quote a fence for the Pat'; orb scales with the audio

Recording panel unmounts; composer returns with 'Reminder for the Hernandez deck' still in the textarea (nothing got sent); no '🎙️' bubble appears in the chat; backend does NOT receive a chat turn or a /files upload
WS to /api/voice/stream closed cleanly with code 1000; no POST to /api/files/files; no POST to /api/agents/chat

evaluationCriteria.check: Cancel × button has enough hit area on touch (≥32px tap target on the mobile breakpoint)
Cancel feels final — there's no confirmation modal or 'are you sure'; the design trusts the user
cancelRecord() clears recChunksRef BEFORE calling recorder.stop() so the onstop handler's chunks.length===0 short-circuit fires; mic stream tracks released
Cancel button has aria-label='Cancel recording'; focus returns to the textarea after cancel
